success_criteria.criterion: A user can create supplier records and import or manually enter products and services with required cost and metadata fields.
A user can organize products, services, bundles, and recipes in a searchable unified asset library.
A user can calculate product, service, labor, shipping, packaging, setup, and other relevant costs into a margin-aware resale price.
A user can build a reusable bundle or production recipe and preserve cost, pricing, and margin visibility.
A user can generate the first validated customer-ready output type from current supplier assets, pricing, bundles, and recipes.

outcome_indicators.indicator: Time from supplier data receipt to customer-ready quote, catalog, or price book decreases versus manual spreadsheet and document workflow.
Users maintain more supplier products and services in Source-Blendr than in standalone spreadsheets after onboarding.
Generated quotes or price books require fewer manual pricing corrections over time.
Low-margin offerings are detected before being sent to customers.

validation_targets_for_stage_2.target: Select and validate the first target vertical and primary buyer profile.
Validate whether file/manual import is acceptable for first product and service aggregation workflows.
Determine the first customer-facing output type: quote, proposal, catalog, price book, or portal view.
Validate the minimum cost breakdown and pricing model required for the first vertical.
Identify the most valuable first bundle or production recipe pattern.
Rank AI, automation, analytics, customer portal, and integration features by validated urgency.
